group_by.note: At most 3 x 2 = 6 groups. Composite key = l_returnflag * 2 + l_linestatus (both dict-encoded small integers). Direct array index, no hash table.

logical_plan.decimal_note: All DECIMAL columns stored as int64_t scaled by 100. Avoid floating-point; compute scaled integer arithmetic throughout. Divide by scale factor only at output.
logical_plan.output_order: ORDER BY l_returnflag, l_linestatus — trivial sort on <=6 result rows, use std::sort on final array.

data_structures.aggregation: flat_array[6] of AggState structs (sum_qty, sum_price, sum_disc_price, sum_charge, count) — one slot per (returnflag, linestatus) group, indexed by composite dict key
data_structures.thread_local_agg: per-thread flat_array[6] to avoid all synchronization in hot loop; merge 64 arrays (384 slots total) sequentially after parallel phase

zone_map_usage.layout: uint32_t num_zones, then per zone: [min:int32_t, max:int32_t, count:uint32_t] (12 bytes/zone), 600 zones
zone_map_usage.skip_condition: zone.min > 10471 → skip entire zone (all rows exceed cutoff)
zone_map_usage.full_pass_condition: zone.max <= 10471 → accept entire zone without per-row check

parallelism.note: Each thread pulls morsels via atomic fetch_add on zone/row counter. Thread-local AggState[6] accumulates without locks. After all morsels processed, main thread merges 64 × 6 = 384 slots.

physical_plan.memory_note: 7 columnar arrays scanned sequentially in parallel morsels. All 7 columns fit within working set for a morsel of 65536 rows: 65536 * 44 bytes = ~2.9 MB per morsel, well within 704 KB L3 per-thread — use smaller morsel of 16384 rows (720 KB) for better cache fit.

index_usage.purpose: zone_map_block_skip — prune ~21 trailing zones (~2.1M rows) where min(l_shipdate) > 10471

physical_plan.late_materialization_note: Not applicable — selectivity is 96.4%, nearly all rows pass the filter. All 7 columns must be read regardless.

estimated_cost.parallel_efficiency: 64 threads on HDD — HDD is likely I/O bound; parallelism helps decompression/decode and CPU-side aggregation but disk throughput is the ceiling
notes: Q1 is I/O-bound on HDD: 60M rows × 44 bytes/row = ~2.6 GB of column data to read
Zone map prunes ~3.5% of data (21 zones × 100K rows)
Aggregation into 6-slot flat array is essentially free (zero hash overhead)
Thread-local merge is trivially cheap (384 scalar additions)
Final sort on <=6 output rows is negligible
Target: match or beat current 108ms GenDB baseline; Umbra/DuckDB achieve 83ms likely with better I/O scheduling or memory-resident data
